## Findings

### F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-001 — The FTK Imager acquisition log for surface_physical records: Ca… *(confidence: HIGH)*

The FTK Imager acquisition log for surface_physical records: Case Number: 20161104; Evidence Number: 20161104-HD001; Unique description: Surface 3; Examiner: Ovie Carroll; Notes: Live Physical; Drive Model: Samsung MDGAGC; and reports the acquisition MD5 checksum 4032d556cc866c23f1e797410e95603c as 'verified' after image verification.. This evidence set is a forensically acquired physical disk image of a Microsoft Surface 3 device, captured live with AccessData FTK Imager under case 20161104 by examiner Ovie Carroll. The matching computed/verification MD5 indicates the image segments verified intact at acquisition time. This establishes provenance and integrity of the image surface only; it says nothing about the system's contents or any activity on it..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/surface_physical.E01.txt#FTK Imager acquisition log` — tool `cat`, command `cat "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/surface_physical.E01.txt"`
- **Cited evidence:** `Case Number: 20161104`, `Evidence Number: 20161104-HD001`, `Unique description: Surface 3`, `Examiner: Ovie Carroll`, `Notes: Live Physical`, `Drive Model: Samsung MDGAGC`, `4032d556cc866c23f1e797410e95603c`, `verified` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 719c061ae75ecc64…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-001`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-001` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `c08029c65be6d22b…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED, scope=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-002 — mmls of surface_physical.E01 reports a GUID Partition Table (EF… *(confidence: HIGH)*

mmls of surface_physical.E01 reports a GUID Partition Table (EFI), units in 512-byte sectors, with slots including: a Basic data partition (start 0000002048), an EFI system partition (start 0000739328), a Microsoft reserved partition (start 0001148928), a large Basic data partition slot 003 spanning start 0001411072 to end 0232294399 (length 0230883328 sectors), and a further Basic data partition (start 0233216000).. The disk uses a GPT layout consistent with a UEFI Windows installation: a small basic-data/recovery partition, an EFI system partition, a Microsoft reserved partition, a large (~0230883328 512-byte sectors, ~110 GiB) basic data partition, and an additional basic data partition. By size, the slot-003 partition starting at sector 1411072 is the primary data partition and the most likely operating-system volume; its filesystem type and contents are not established by mmls alone..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/surface_physical.E01#GPT partition table (mmls)` — tool `mmls`, command `mmls "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/surface_physical.E01"`
- **Cited evidence:** `GUID Partition Table (EFI)`, `EFI system partition`, `Microsoft reserved partition`, `Basic data partition`, `0001411072`, `0232294399`, `0230883328` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 9f55f4dff2d8f8c7…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-002`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-002` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `92cb15e3d8552f53…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED, scope=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-003 — fls -o 1411072 of surface_physical.E01 lists the root directory… *(confidence: HIGH)*

fls -o 1411072 of surface_physical.E01 lists the root directory of the partition. Entries include NTFS metadata files $MFT, $MFTMirr, $Boot, $LogFile, $Bitmap, $Secure, $UpCase, and $RECYCLE.BIN, alongside directories Windows, Windows.old, Users, ProgramData, Program Files, Program Files (x86), Recovery, $Windows.~GWX and $Windows.~WS, and files hiberfil.sys, pagefile.sys and swapfile.sys.. The slot-003 partition at sector offset 1411072 is an NTFS filesystem (presence of $MFT, $Boot, $LogFile, $RECYCLE.BIN) hosting a Windows operating system (Windows, ProgramData, Program Files, Users directories). A separate 'Windows.old' directory plus '$Windows.~GWX'/'$Windows.~WS' staging directories indicate a prior Windows installation was retained, consistent with an in-place Windows upgrade/reinstall on this volume. hiberfil.sys/pagefile.sys/swapfile.sys are present. This identifies and characterises the OS volume; it does not establish any user, account, execution, or activity..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/surface_physical.E01#NTFS root @ partition offset 1411072 (fls)` — tool `fls`, command `fls -o 1411072 "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/surface_physical.E01"`
- **Cited evidence:** `$MFT`, `$Boot`, `$LogFile`, `$RECYCLE.BIN`, `Windows`, `Windows.old`, `Users`, `ProgramData`, `Program Files`, `Program Files (x86)`, `$Windows.~GWX`, `hiberfil.sys`, `pagefile.sys` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 ce12c9df5f96739f…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-003`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-003` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `06897047d366f9dd…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED, scope=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-004 — 7z l of vanko-c-drive.CYLR.7z reports Type = 7z and lists a top… *(confidence: HIGH)*

7z l of vanko-c-drive.CYLR.7z reports Type = 7z and lists a top-level folder 'vanko-c-drive.CYLR' containing a volume root 'vanko-c-drive.CYLR/G' under which Windows directory structures are preserved (e.g. ProgramData/Microsoft/Search and Users/<profile>/AppData paths). The archive summary line reports '6576 files, 1750 folders'.. The archive is a CYLR-style live-response triage collection (its top-level folder uses the '.CYLR' naming convention of the CYLR collection tool) gathered from a Windows volume that was referenced as drive letter G at collection time and labelled 'vanko-c-drive'. It preserves the original directory tree of selected forensic locations (search index, user AppData/browser profiles, etc.) and contains 6576 files across 1750 folders. Entry timestamps inside the archive are file metadata and do not by themselves establish when collection occurred or any user activity. This characterises the scope of the triage dataset only..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/vanko-c-drive.CYLR.7z#CYLR triage archive listing (7z l)` — tool `7z`, command `7z l "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/vanko-c-drive.CYLR.7z"`
- **Cited evidence:** `Type = 7z`, `vanko-c-drive.CYLR`, `vanko-c-drive.CYLR/G`, `ProgramData`, `Users`, `6576 files, 1750 folders` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 35b026d2da8ff27b…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-004`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-004` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `cb098347af9b8b3b…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED, scope=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-006 — fls of the Users directory (inode 81647) on the partition at of… *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

fls of the Users directory (inode 81647) on the partition at offset 1411072 lists the following entries: All Users, Default, Default User, Default.migrated, defaultprinter, desktop.ini, PC User, and Public.. The Users directory listing contains standard system/template profiles (All Users, Default, Default User, Public), a 'Default.migrated' directory (a name typically produced when the default profile is migrated during an OS upgrade/reinstall), and two named profile directories 'PC User' and 'defaultprinter'. These entries reflect profile directories present in the filesystem listing; on their own they do not indicate account ownership, who logged in, login activity, or any user action. The 'Default.migrated' entry is consistent with the in-place upgrade also indicated by the Windows.old directory.. This is the **corrected** finding — an initial, stronger reading was refuted by the Council (see *Self-correction*, below) and the analyst re-filed only what the evidence supports.

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/surface_physical.E01#Users profile directory (fls inode 81647 @ offset 1411072)` — tool `fls`, command `fls -o 1411072 "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case 2/extracted/surface_physical.E01" 81…`
- **Cited evidence:** `All Users`, `Default`, `Default User`, `Default.migrated`, `defaultprinter`, `desktop.ini`, `PC User`, `Public` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 7d3931eace864299…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-006`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-006` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `710832ff9e327401…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED, scope=SUPPORTED

## What the evidence does NOT support (Council-enforced discipline)

The Council refused the following stronger readings because the available artifacts do not establish them.
They are recorded here explicitly so the report distinguishes **confirmed** from **inferred or unfounded**:

- Inference overreach (CERTAINTY): unjustified certainty — a single artifact rarely proves a conclusion outright. Offending clause: "This establishes that these profile folders exist on disk".

## Self-correction record (no human intervention)

- **F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-005** was drafted as: *"fls of the Users directory (inode 81647) on the partition at offset 1411072 lis… → The Windows volume contains the user-profile directories: standard system/template profil…"*, then **bounced** by the Council:
  > [seat:inference] Inference overreach (CERTAINTY): unjustified certainty — a single artifact rarely proves a conclusion outright. Offending clause: "This establishes that these profile folders exist on disk".

  The analyst read the objection and self-corrected — re-filing the supported claim now shown under *Findings*.

## Confidence & limitations

- Confidence is stated per finding (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / SPECULATIVE) and reflects what a single artifact
  can bear; nothing here is asserted beyond the cited evidence.
- "Observation" is the literal tool output; "interpretation" is the analyst's inference. The Council's
  Citation, Tool-semantics, Contradiction, Inference, and Scope seats independently checked the leap between them; Synthesis aggregated the verdicts.
- Every finding traces to its tool execution (`csift trace F-analyst-VANKO-AUTO-001`); concrete-command
  receipts can additionally be re-executed against evidence with `csift trace --rerun …`. Council Receipts are hash-chained.
- This narrative is generated from the audit substrate, not hand-written — it cannot contain a claim that
  is not a verified node in the graph.
